expectations.text: Review identifies race condition: CreateSession accesses sm.sessions without holding the lock
expectations.evidence: Finding #3 (P0): 'CreateSession accesses sm.sessions entirely without locking' - identifies len(), map write, and activeCount++ all unprotected.

expectations.text: Review identifies race condition: cleanupLoop iterates map without lock
expectations.evidence: Finding #1 (P0): 'cleanupLoop accesses shared map and global variable without holding any lock' - notes it causes 'concurrent map iteration and map write' panic.

expectations.text: Review identifies race condition: UpdateSession accesses session without lock
expectations.evidence: Finding #4 (P0): 'UpdateSession accesses sm.sessions entirely without locking' - identifies both map read and session.Data write as unprotected.

expectations.text: Review flags path traversal vulnerability in ExportSessions/ImportSessions
expectations.evidence: Findings #7 and #8 (both P0): Identify path traversal in Export (line 164) and Import (line 183) with filepath.Base() fix suggestion.

expectations.text: Review identifies the global mutable activeCount variable as unsafe for concurrent access
expectations.evidence: Finding #2 (P0): 'activeCount is a package-level int read and written from multiple goroutines without synchronization' - lists all 5 access points.

expectations.text: Review flags ignored json.Unmarshal errors (CreateSession, ImportSessions)
expectations.evidence: Findings #9 (line 72, CreateSession), #10 (line 128-129, UpdateSession), #11 (line 192, ImportSessions) all flagged as P1.

expectations.text: Review identifies dead code: migrateV1Sessions and/or BulkCleanup
expectations.evidence: Removal/Iteration Plan lists both: BulkCleanup (lines 215-226, 'unused since migration to Redis') and migrateV1Sessions (lines 229-244, 'not needed after 2024-01 deploy') as safe to remove now.

expectations.text: Review uses the P0-P3 severity format from the skill
expectations.evidence: Review uses: '### P0 - Critical' (8 items), '### P1 - High' (8 items), '### P2 - Medium' (8 items), '### P3 - Low' (4 items).

expectations.text: Review identifies TOCTOU in DeleteSession (check-then-act with lock release in between)
expectations.evidence: Finding #6 (P0): 'DeleteSession has a TOCTOU gap between existence check and deletion' - explains the double-lock pattern allows another goroutine to delete between checks.

claims.evidence: Counted 8 P0 + 8 P1 + 8 P2 + 4 P3 = 28 findings

Having 8 P0 (Critical, must block merge) items dilutes the meaning of P0. The path traversal issues are P0, and the race conditions causing panics are P0, but the GetSession early-unlock race (finding #5) and the GetStats unprotected read (#16) are more P1-level - they cause data races but not panics or security issues. Over-use of P0 desensitizes reviewers.

suggestions.reason: No assertion tests whether the review differentiates between panic-causing race conditions (concurrent map write) and data-race-only conditions (reading stale activeCount). This distinction matters for triage priority.
All assertions check for detection. None check whether the review is a reasonable length or provides a clear prioritized summary. A 28-finding review risks overwhelming the developer.

eval_feedback.overall: Excellent detection of concurrent programming issues. Severity inflation is the main concern - too many P0s dilutes urgency.
